name: Artillery (Modern Load Testing Platform)
pricing_notes: Open source core (MPL-2.0); Artillery Cloud (managed runs, CI integration) is commercial

auth.notes: Artillery Cloud uses API key; test targets use whatever auth the API needs

performance.notes: Artillery worker generates load; can scale to millions of VUs via cloud

agent_readiness.notes: YAML-driven load testing with JavaScript hooks. Scenarios defined in YAML with HTTP, WebSocket, Socket.IO, gRPC, Playwright browser engines. Custom functions in JS. `artillery run test.yml`. Metrics: latency histograms, error rates, throughput. Artillery Cloud for distributed cloud execution. Plugins for DataDog, CloudWatch, Slack. Check HTTP responses with `expect`. Synthetic monitoring (schedule regular runs). Better multi-protocol support than Locust. Less code-heavy than k6 for simple cases.

gotchas: YAML config verbose for complex scenarios
Node.js single-process limits throughput for extreme loads
Artillery Cloud required for large distributed loads
best_when: Multi-protocol load testing (HTTP, WebSocket, gRPC in one tool)
Teams wanting YAML-driven simple tests with JS escape hatch
Synthetic monitoring (scheduled performance tests in CI)
Browser-based load testing via Playwright integration
avoid_when: Extreme high-throughput loads (k6 or Gatling better)
Pure HTTP testing by Python teams (Locust more ergonomic)
Simple curl-based load testing (hey or vegeta simpler)
summary: Artillery is a modern YAML-driven load testing tool with HTTP, WebSocket, gRPC, and Playwright browser engines. Scenarios in YAML with JavaScript hooks. Synthetic monitoring via scheduled runs. Artillery Cloud for distributed execution. Best for multi-protocol load tests and teams wanting simple YAML configuration with JavaScript extensibility. k6 is preferred for JavaScript-native or high-throughput scenarios.
